title: The Rota Greenway · a former railway, now a walking and cycling route
meta_description: The Rota Greenway: about 7.5 km along the old railway line that linked Rota with El Puerto de Santa María, now an accessible, family-friendly blue trail. A treasure a short walk from the corrales.

hero.sub: Old railway tracks turned into a route for walking, running or cycling. Easy, flat and very family-friendly.
hero.img_alt: A cyclist riding the Rota Greenway between trees

sections.lead: The Greenway reclaims the old railway line that once linked Rota with El Puerto de Santa María.
paras: It runs for about 7.5 kilometres and is recognised as a «blue trail». It crosses farmland, small stands of trees, streams and native vegetation, with rest areas along the way.

Families love it for its accessibility: a minimal gradient and a wide surface make it comfortable on foot, by bike or in a group. In the episode of «Más allá del mar», Andrés Barba rides it with Carolina Bonhomo, of the Environment Department at Rota Town Council.

sections.title: The Rota Greenway: an essential route in Cádiz
sections.desc: Andrés Barba and Carolina Bonhomo travel the Rota Greenway and tell its history and its value. In Spanish.
sections.alt: The Rota Greenway path running through vegetation

sections.h2: Pair it with a visit to the corrales
